description_long: This is a hand trowel with a pointed, diamond-shaped metal blade and a cylindrical wooden handle. The blade is dark, likely made of steel, and is attached to the handle with a short, angled neck. The handle appears to be made of wood, showing a natural brown color and a smooth, slightly worn texture. The blade is flat and tapers to a sharp point, suitable for precise digging or spreading tasks. The overall construction is robust, with the handle and blade securely joined by a metal ferrule. No visible brand markings or mounting holes are present on the handle or blade.
description: A hand trowel with a pointed, diamond-shaped steel blade and a cylindrical wooden handle, joined by a metal ferrule.
description_view_0: Front view showing the pointed, diamond-shaped blade facing forward and the cylindrical wooden handle behind.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the angled neck connecting the blade to the handle and the full length of the wooden grip.
description_view_2: Top-down view displaying the width of the handle and the full surface area of the blade.
description_view_3: Opposite side view similar to view 1, showing the angled neck and the handle's length.
attribute_handle_type: Straight cylindrical wooden handle with a smooth finish and no visible grip enhancements.
attribute_blade_type: Diamond-shaped flat steel blade with a pointed tip and straight edges.
